ocr.text: The table outlines various instruments and assessment areas utilized in a research context, detailing the time of assessments and
the individuals applying these instruments. The first instrument, the Sampling form assistant, assesses age, sex, and
inclusion/exclusion criteria at Baseline, applied by Research. The second instrument, the Sociodemographic data form assistant,
examines age, sex, marital status, and educational level at Baseline, also applied by Research. The table includes a reference to
socio-economic group [29] and occupation, but this has no associated instrument or application. The SPPI psychiatric interview,
administered by a psychiatrist [30], focuses on psychiatric diagnosis at Baseline, under Research. The Pain catastrophizing scale
[20] assistant evaluates the severity of catastrophizing during Baseline and follow-up sessions*, with oversight by Research.
The Hamilton test for Depression assistant [31,32] assesses severity of depression at Baseline and follow-up sessions*,
managed by Research.
Instrument  Assessment area Time(s) of assessments  Applied by
Sampling form assistant  Age, sex, inclusion/exclusion criteria Baseline  Research
Sociodemographic data form assistant  Age, sex, marital status, educational level, Baseline  Research
Socio-economic group [29], occupation
SPPI psychiatric interview psychiatrist [30]  Psychiatric diagnosis Baseline  Research
Pain catastrophizing scale [20] assistant  Severity of catastrophizing Baseline and follow-up sessions*  Research
Hamilton test for Depression assistant [31,32]  Severity of depression Baseline and follow-up sessions*  Research
Hamilton test for Anxiety assistant [33,34]  Severity of anxiety Baseline and follow-up sessions*  Research
Fibromyalgia Impact assistant Questionnaire [35,36]  General function Baseline and follow-up sessions*  Research
Medical record assistant  Pharmacological Follow-up sessions* side-effect events  Research
EQ-5D [37] assistant  Health related quality of life Baseline and follow-up sessions*  Research
CSRI [38] assistant  Health and social services use Baseline and follow-up sessions*  Research
The Hamilton test for Anxiety assistant [33,34] measures severity of anxiety at Baseline and follow-up sessions*, conducted by
Research. The Fibromyalgia Impact Questionnaire [35,36] assistant reviews general function at Baseline and follow-up
sessions*, also in a research setting. The Medical record assistant evaluates pharmacological side-effect events during follow-up
sessions*, again under Research. The EQ-5D [37] assistant assesses health-related quality of life at Baseline and follow-up
sessions*, facilitated by Research. Lastly, the CSRI [38] assistant reviews health and social services use at Baseline and follow-
up sessions*, conducted under Research. Each instrument contributes comprehensive data relevant to the study’s objectives.
